reflection:
The sciatica crash yesterday was your body giving you a clear message. Pain went from 3 to 6, energy collapsed after lunch, and you spent the afternoon icing and resting. That's the direct aftermath of a heavy treatment week, two sessions with Beata, Renzo, a lot of bodywork in a few days. The system needed to process.

The worry you wrote is honest and worth listening to. "Doing too much, exercises might overload my lower back." And then you walked 16,000 steps and went to Harrods anyway. Not a criticism, but there's a pattern there, knowing what you need and doing something else.

Here's what matters though. Ten days off amitriptyline, and this morning's check-in had a different quality. Funny dreams, waking up feeling alive, things working that the medication was quietly muffling. That's a real data point about what life looks like on the other side of the taper. Pay attention to it.

One thing today: nothing new. No exercises, no testing the lower back, no adding input. Let the work from last week actually land. The treatments happened, now the body needs time to integrate.

The chicken at Lima Cantina sounds like it was exactly right.
opener: Sciatica flare after a heavy treatment week - body processing
focus_for_today: No new exercises or activities today - let last week's bodywork settle
